lever_summary: Replace the FxHashMap<VarId,Value> interpreter environment with a flat Vec<Option<Value>> indexed by VarId.0 — a different memory layout giving O(1) array-indexed variable lookups and a single allocation instead of an incrementally-grown hash map. Adds an allocation-free reused input-scratch buffer and a direct single-output eval_primitive dispatch. A sparse/huge variable-id fallback retains the original FxHashMap path with identical semantics.
method: Same-worker RCH Criterion. Baseline = pre-change FxHashMap interpreter (pass90 profile). Calibration anchor = vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 (few-equation, kernel-dominated; ~unaffected by the env change) to bound worker variance.

change.lever: eval_jaxpr_with_consts computes max defined var id + def count; if ids are dense (slots <= def_count*8 max 256) it runs eval_jaxpr_dense_env (flat Vec<Option<Value>>, reused scratch, direct single-output eval), else eval_jaxpr_hashed_env (original FxHashMap). eval_equation_outputs was split into resolve (env-dependent) + eval_equation_outputs_from_resolved (shared by both env backends) so sub-jaxpr/multi-output dispatch is identical.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: input atoms resolved in order; equations evaluated in order; outvars collected in order
isomorphism.dispatch: single-output non-subjaxpr prims call eval_primitive directly == eval_primitive_multi's vec![eval_primitive] for non-multi prims (gated on outputs.len()==1); multi-output (Qr/Lu/Svd/Eigh/TopK/Slogdet/Eig) and sub-jaxpr equations route through eval_equation_outputs_from_resolved
isomorphism.errors: MissingVariable on dense bounds-checked get()==None matches HashMap get()==None; arity checks unchanged
isomorphism.fallback: sparse/huge ids use the original FxHashMap path; dense_and_hashed_envs_agree proves the two paths are bit-identical

validation.name: dense_hashed_parity_and_suite
validation.result: 131 passed incl dense_and_hashed_envs_agree (dense vs sparse-id hashed env produce bit-identical outputs)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-interpreters --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: 1.74x (consistent 1.75x @100) is below a literal 2x but is a structural memory-layout REPLACEMENT (not a micro-tweak) on the universal interpreter hot loop, with ICE Score 10.0 and calibration-confirmed real effect. The remaining ~67ns/eqn is eval_primitive dispatch itself; exceeding 2x requires a compiled interpreter (flat opcode/fn-pointer stream + register-allocated slots) — filed as the next bead.
